event1.text: I found a route under the relay spine.

event13.text: I'm already regretting saying those words.

event5.text: I don't want to go in there.

event7.text: Which means we probably have to.

backgroundEvent8.text: Maya, if the archive still has power, assume it still has teeth.

backgroundEvent15.text: Hold on. Something shifted under the panel.

backgroundEvent16.text: No. I'm moving. Not testing my luck by standing still.

event7.text: If I go quiet, I'm busy not dying.
I'll report at the archive hatch.

backgroundEvent20.text: Cable touched my neck. I handled it normally. Very normally.

event3.text: It wants manual open or safety scan.

backgroundEvent9.text: I thought I heard something behind the hatch.

backgroundEvent10.text: No. Just the relay pack settling. I think.

event7.text: Or spend a minute making sure it doesn't bite.

event22.text: Just enough to make me personally dislike this door.

event3.text: Keeping one hand off the panel.

event5.text: Hale, if this thing eats my fingerprints, I'm blaming you retroactively.

backgroundEvent6.text: Careful is not cowardly. I know that ruins Maya's entire brand, but there it is.

event7.text: And an empty slot tagged VOSS.

event16.text: But the trauma files are alphabetized.

backgroundEvent17.text: Hold on. Something moved between the cabinets.

backgroundEvent18.text: No. Hanging label. It swung when I stepped in. I hate this room.

event9.text: The VOSS drawer has no handle.

event28.text: Just a clean rectangle where one used to be.

event0.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - ETHAN HALE
event0.content:
Subject: Ethan Hale
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject demonstrates strong command resilience under isolation pressure.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
event0.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Timestamp appears inconsistent.

event9.text: I once threatened a printer with decompression.

event27.text: I don't know why I know it.

event0.title: PSYCHOLOGICAL EVALUATION - MAYA CHEN
event0.content:
Subject: Maya Chen
Status: Stable
Risk Level: Low
Recommendation: Active Duty Approved
Evaluator Note: Subject shows high pattern-recognition ability and repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
Created: 12 April
Linked Incident: Alpha
event0.caption: Recovered psychological evaluation record. Note contains memory suppression reference.

event4.text: Nobody told us there were memory protocols.

event18.text: And someone wrote it down like medicine.

event4.text: Before I start making up a version that hurts less.

event11.text: And if you opened mine first...

event5.text: If I spiral, talk to me like I'm fixing something.

event0.title: PSYCH EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY
event0.content:
HALE: Marked stable after Incident Alpha.
MAYA: Repeated resistance to memory suppression protocols.
VOSS: Archive slot exists, record body removed.
INDEX: Archive created after Incident Alpha date.

Conflict: Evaluations appear to be post-incident control records, not pre-incident wellness assessments.
event0.caption: Combined evaluation conflict summary generated from recovered records.
event0.message: [EVALUATION CONFLICT SUMMARY ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.text: They didn't want to know if we were okay.

event4.text: They wanted the file to say we were.

event3.text: And if Hale gets calm again, remember this room.

event8.text: That sounds like something people say when they want control.

event13.text: Some systems wake up when you look at them.

event15.text: This archive may be one of them.

backgroundEvent8.text: You hear that? He made fear sound like advice.

event12.text: Easier for the archive to lie through.

event15.text: Or I pull the original index drive.

backgroundEvent6.text: If it asks twice, read the second prompt. Old systems hide knives in confirmation screens.

event5.text: I can feel it through the glove.

event14.text: If I go quiet, both hands are busy.

backgroundEvent15.text: Wait. I thought I saw a face in the cabinet glass.

backgroundEvent16.text: No. Mine. Reflection. Hate that face right now.

event0.title: GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT
event0.content:
OFFICIAL MANIFEST COUNT: 6
RECOVERED MANIFEST LAYER COUNT: 7
ORIGINAL ASSIGNMENT INDEX COUNT: 8

Discrepancy: one person removed from official manifest. One additional person removed from recovered manifest layer.
Visual archive unavailable. Count derived from personnel hashes, suit registry keys and life support allocation records.
event0.caption: Personnel count reconstructed without image records.
event0.message: [GHOST CREW COUNT REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event17.text: I need that sentence to be wrong.

event3.text: And I don't like how close that feels to a memory.

backgroundEvent8.text: Operator... sealed subfolder under the eighth slot.

seal_eighth_slot.text: SEAL IT AND GET MAYA OUT.

event3.title: EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT
event3.content:
Assignment Index Slot: 08
Name Field: CORRUPTED
Suit Registry Echo: NV-08 adjacent
Pod Link: External emergency pod
Access Note: DO NOT RESTORE WHILE SUBJECT IS BREATHING
event3.caption: Fragment recovered from the sealed eighth assignment slot. Opening this record exposed the pod link.
event3.message: [EIGHTH SLOT FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event8.text: If this is a mistake, it is one of ours.

event5.text: Because part of me is relieved.

event7.text: And I don't know what that says about me.

backgroundEvent15.text: I think something opened a drawer behind me.

event1.text: I'm getting out before I change my mind.

event3.text: If I ask to go back in, say no.

event5.text: If I get angry, still say no.

event4.text: I am so tired of things remembering for us.

event6.text: Then we go to the pod.

event10.text: You always say that right before everything gets worse.

event16.text: This time I am hoping to be wrong.

event20.text: Found scorch marks on the spine.

event25.title: DIRECT SPINE BURN TRACE
event25.content:
DIRECT RELAY SPINE TRACE
Residual heat pattern detected near archive access.
Pattern shape: emergency pod transfer conduit.
Age estimate: recent / post-blackout.
Note: Route was used after official shutdown.
event25.caption: Heat trace found on the direct spine. Suggests pod-related activity after the blackout.
event25.message: [DIRECT SPINE BURN TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event10.text: There are boot prints in the dust.

event15.text: Someone came through after power failed.

event22.text: I hate that I had to add that.

event23.title: CRAWLSPACE DRAG TRACE
event23.content:
SERVICE CRAWL TRACE
Boot pattern: unknown
Drag mark: narrow, intermittent
Direction: archive access hatch → auxiliary pod conduit
Timestamp: post-blackout estimate
event23.caption: Physical trace discovered in the service crawlspace. Suggests someone moved through after power loss.
event23.message: [CRAWLSPACE DRAG TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event8.text: The kind that pretends it's furniture.

event12.text: Hale was right about the second prompt.

event21.text: Remote report has a checksum gap.
It has a raw assignment hash.

event24.title: RAW ASSIGNMENT HASH
event24.content:
ORIGINAL INDEX DRIVE HASH
Slot 08: active key preserved
Name field: stripped
Pod conduit key: present
Archive mirror: absent
Note: Raw index predates psych archive overwrite.
event24.caption: Raw assignment hash recovered only by physically removing the index drive.
event24.message: [RAW ASSIGNMENT HASH ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event11.text: I am trying not to remember something that wants to be remembered.

event13.text: Lights just came on behind me.

event9.text: Don't ask me to make that make sense.

event12.text: Which makes me trust it less.

event13.title: SEALED EIGHTH SLOT TRACE
event13.content:
SEALED SLOT PRESSURE TRACE
Slot: 08
Name field: inaccessible
External link: emergency pod conduit
Safety state: sealed / not awakened
Note: Opening deferred. Trace preserved without activating archive response.
event13.caption: Trace preserved by sealing the eighth slot instead of opening it. The pod link is visible, but the name remains hidden.
event13.message: [SEALED EIGHTH SLOT TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event10.text: Something knocked inside the archive after I crossed the hatch.

event12.text: Like drawers opening one at a time.

event14.text: You told me to open it.

event17.text: I'm not saying that like blame.

event19.text: I'm saying it because I need you to remember.

event14.text: Or saved a person from being woken wrong.
